author: Her

capabilities.description: Create a local mini web app from a complete single-file HTML document. The app is hosted on a loopback-only server with its own SQLite database. Inside the HTML, call the data API with: fetch('api/query?token=' + new URLSearchParams(location.search).get('token'), {method:'POST', body: JSON.stringify({sql:'...', params:[...]})}). The response is {ok, columns, rows, rows_changed, last_insert_row_id}. Optionally pass backend_type (node|python) plus backend_code to add a backend process: the script must start an HTTP server on 127.0.0.1 at the PORT environment variable (HER_WEBAPP_DB has the SQLite path, standard library only, no npm/pip installs). The page reaches it via fetch('backend/<route>?token=' + token). Keep everything inline (no external CDN scripts or styles).

backend_code.description: Optional backend script source. Must listen on 127.0.0.1 at env PORT using only the standard library.

backend_type.description: Optional backend runtime: node or python.

description.description: One-line summary of what the app does.

html.description: Complete self-contained HTML document (inline CSS/JS, no external resources).

name.description: Human-readable app name shown in the Apps page.

capabilities.title: Create local web app

capabilities.description: Replace the HTML of an installed local web app, and optionally rename it or replace its backend script. The app keeps its SQLite data; a replaced backend restarts on the next request.

app_id.description: ID of the installed web app (see webapp.list).

backend_code.description: Optional replacement backend script (see webapp.create for the contract).

backend_type.description: Optional backend runtime when replacing backend_code: node or python.

capabilities.title: Update local web app

capabilities.description: List installed local web apps with their ids, names, and local URLs.
capabilities.title: List local web apps

capabilities.description: Open an installed local web app in the Apps page so the user can see it.

app_id.description: ID of the installed web app (see webapp.list).

capabilities.title: Open local web app

capabilities.description: Delete an installed local web app, including its SQLite data.

app_id.description: ID of the installed web app (see webapp.list).

capabilities.title: Remove local web app

description: Vibe-code small local web apps: single-file HTML hosted on a loopback server with per-app SQLite storage, shown in the Apps page as embedded pages.
name: WebApp Runtime
systemPromptAddendum: Local web apps are self-contained single-file HTML documents served from a loopback-only server. Each app has its own SQLite database reachable from page JavaScript via POST api/query with the token from the page URL query string. Apps may optionally declare a node or python backend process (standard library only) that listens on 127.0.0.1 at env PORT; the page reaches it via fetch('backend/<route>?token=' + token) and HER_WEBAPP_DB gives the backend the SQLite path. Prefer the static + SQLite form; add a backend only when page JavaScript is not enough. Creating, updating, and removing apps requires user approval — mention clearly in your reply when an app will run a local backend process. After webapp.create succeeds, call webapp.open so the user sees the app immediately.
